Domestic Violence Protection

Overview

We provide robust legal advocacy for protection, residence orders, and compensation in domestic violence cases. We represent both survivors seeking urgent protection and individuals defending against false allegations.

Key Areas of Focus

  • ✓ Filing Protection Orders & Residence Orders
  • ✓ Monetary Relief & Compensation Claims
  • ✓ Interim Relief Applications
  • ✓ Defense against false DV allegations

Primary Legislation & Statutes

Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973

Frequently Asked Questions

Any woman who is, or has been, in a shared household with the respondent and alleges to have been subjected to any form of domestic violence (physical, emotional, sexual, or economic) by the respondent can file a complaint.

The magistrate can pass interim protection orders, direct the respondent to pay monetary relief, secure alternative accommodation or pass residence orders, and grant temporary custody of children.

Need Legal Assistance?

Schedule a confidential consultation with our legal experts today.
